theme: a body plan that survives losing a gene
title: The Gene You Can Lose
strategies: mode-guess baseline: ignore position and knockouts entirely, always output the single most common target type (reproduces the evaluator's weak reference)

direct positional address: one gene per positional bit mirroring that bit exactly, with a lookup table fit to reproduce the wild-type target perfectly -- exact but has zero redundancy, so any single-gene knockout silently relabels roughly half the body to a different position's identity

duplicated / paralog encoding: every positional bit is mirrored onto TWO genes (paralogs); losing either twin still leaves the bit legible via its sibling, so the full positional address -- and hence the target match -- survives every single-gene knockout with no information loss
seeded local search over regulatory weights/decode tables to trade off wild-type fit against redundancy under a fixed gene budget
